title: INtech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: How Curling Became the Winter Olympics’ Favorite Fixation
items.description: If throwing stones and sweeping ice seem strange to you, the centuries-old history of the sport explains why it became so beloved.

items.title: AI Is Here to Replace Nuclear Treaties. Scared Yet?

items.title: Oticon Zeal Review: A High Bar (and Price) for Hearing Aids
items.description: These ultra-tiny hearing aids push the boundary for high-quality audio in a minuscule prescription package.

items.title: Bryte Balance Pro Smart Mattress Review: Sleeping Is Believing
items.description: The Bryte Balance Pro takes smart mattresses to the next level with adjustable coils and programmable massage settings.

items.title: Iran’s Digital Surveillance Machine Is Almost Complete

items.title: Amazon’s ‘Melania’ documentary stumbles in second weekend
items.description: After a better-than-expected opening weekend in theaters, box office for Amazon’s “Melania”fell 67%.

items.title: San Francisco’s pro-billionaire march draws dozens
items.description: A march supporting California’s billionaires didn’t exactly draw a huge crowd on Saturday.

items.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Is $16B enough to build a profitable robotaxi business?
items.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

items.title: Crypto.com places $70M bet on AI.com domain ahead of Super Bowl
items.description: The purchase rewrites the domain record books -- not that the crypto industry has ever been accused of restraint when it comes to spending.
